pattern: Three consecutive integers always sum to three times the middle one, so the whole search reduces to a single divisibility check and a division.
hints: Call the middle of the three integers x; the other two are just x minus one and x plus one.
Adding all three together always collapses to exactly three times that middle value.
If the target is not evenly divisible by three there is no answer at all; otherwise divide by three to get the middle value directly.

edge_inputs.description: smallest positive number divisible by three
a small number not divisible by three
another small number not divisible by three
a number divisible by three where the middle integer is itself divisible by three
the largest allowed input, divisible by three
the largest allowed input, when it is even and not divisible by three
a mid-sized number just short of a multiple of three

stress.description: random values across the full bound, mixing divisible and non-divisible cases
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    hi = max(1, min(size, 1000000000000000))
    num = rng.randint(0, hi)
    return [num]
